one_liner: LLY -0.64% to $1,191 on declining volume — a Wyckoff LPS (Last Point of Support) event tagged today as the stock pulls back from last Thursday's high-volume Sign of Strength, with Phase E (Complete) and DCF showing 39.8% upside.

price_action.vol_rank: Below average — volume drying on pullback is bullish
price_action.intraday_story: LLY opened at $1,217 (slightly above Thursday's close), briefly touched $1,215.80 (high slightly below open — an inside-bar start), then sold off to $1,173.93 low before recovering to close at $1,191.74. The 0.77x below-average volume on a -41-point intraday drop is the key positive signal: when a stock pulls back -3.6% from high to low on 23% below-average volume, sellers are not present — this is a supply-absence pullback, the ideal setup before a continuation.

news_catalyst.headline: No major news today — LLY digesting last Thursday's 2.35x high-volume surge from $1,128 to $1,208
news_catalyst.summary:
Last Thursday (June 26), LLY surged from $1,128 to $1,208 on 2.35x volume — the second-highest volume day in the 90-day dataset. That explosive move was almost certainly a Sign of Strength (SOS) driven by positive GLP-1/weight-loss drug data or an analyst upgrade (the 2.35x volume is strong institutional buying evidence). Today's low-volume pullback ($1,173-$1,215 range, 0.77x volume) is exactly the 'Last Point of Support' (LPS) behavior that Wyckoff Phase E describes: a minor retracement on drying volume that creates the final low-risk entry before the next advance.

LLY's fundamentals around Zepbound and Mounjaro (GLP-1 drugs) remain the strongest growth narrative in large-cap pharma. The Wyckoff LPS tag today is algorithmically generated from the pattern — it reinforces rather than replaces the fundamental bullish thesis.

technical.summary:
LLY is tagged as 'Accumulation Phase E (Complete)' — in Wyckoff methodology, Phase E is the final phase of accumulation where the stock breaks out of the accumulation range and enters a markup phase. 'Complete' suggests the engine has identified the transition from consolidation to uptrend.

The LPS event tagged today means the algorithm has detected a low-volume pullback to support after a high-volume breakout — precisely the pattern described above. The June 29 high of $1,238 is the nearest resistance; the June 26 SOS day established $1,128-$1,208 as the new base. Today's low of $1,173 held well above the June 26 open of $1,128 — structural integrity intact.

DCF shows $1,980 intrinsic value vs $1,191 price — 39.8% margin of safety. For a quality pharma franchise with the GLP-1 market opportunity ahead, this is an unusually attractive valuation.

wyckoff_narrative:
LLY's Wyckoff Phase E (Complete) designation means the algorithm has identified a completed accumulation cycle. Today's LPS (Last Point of Support) tag is the final piece: after the SOS on June 26 (2.35x volume, strong up day), the stock is experiencing a brief consolidation/pullback on drying volume before the next leg higher.

In Phase E, LPS events are high-conviction entry signals — they represent the last opportunity to enter before the markup extends. The blueprint: the June 26 SOS cleared the range, June 29-30 consolidated (1.10x volume), and today's LPS (0.77x volume on a pullback) sets the stage. The playbook says the next 5-10 sessions should see LLY advance toward the $1,238+ zone and beyond. A break back below $1,173 on elevated volume would invalidate the Phase E reading.

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $1,980 implies 39.8% upside from $1,191 current — a substantial margin of safety for a mega-cap pharma name. The GLP-1 market (Zepbound/Mounjaro) represents a multi-decade growth runway that may make even the $1,980 DCF base conservative. The bull case is very wide given obesity drug adoption uncertainty.

key_strengths: Market leader in GLP-1 obesity/diabetes drugs — Zepbound and Mounjaro
DCF shows 39.8% upside at current levels
Massive R&D pipeline with multiple blockbuster candidates
key_concerns: $1,191 price creates liquidity constraints for position sizing
GLP-1 competition from Novo Nordisk's Wegovy remains fierce
Government drug pricing negotiations (Medicare) represent regulatory risk
fundamentals.summary: LLY is fundamentally one of the strongest companies in the watchlist. The GLP-1 franchise is the biggest pharmaceutical growth story of the decade. DCF supports 39.8% upside. The Wyckoff LPS + Phase E Complete alignment makes this the clearest accumulate setup in the pre-market scan today.

risks: If LLY breaks below the June 26 SOS day open of $1,128 on elevated volume, the Phase E reading is wrong and the stock needs to rebuild its base
Drug pricing executive orders from the Trump administration have historically caused sharp single-day LLY selloffs
Position sizing: at $1,191/share, a 1% position in a portfolio requires ~$12/100k — plan lot sizes carefully
